findings.evidence: `CoerciveEnergy.lean:126` and `UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:165` expose explicit lower-bound lemmas; `rg -n "add_principal_mass|min_coercivityConstant.*add" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE` finds only `CoercivePerturbation.lean:48` and `:66`, no explicit perturbed lower-bound lemma.
findings.fix: Add a perturbed version of `min_coercivityConstant_mul_norm_sq_le_energyIntegrand_self`, with the same constant and perturbed right-hand side, and annotate it consistently with the existing coercive-energy API.
findings.issue: `isCoercive_energyIntegrand_add_principal_mass_of_isCoercive` gives only existential `IsCoercive`, so downstream Lax--Milgram/Gårding code cannot retain the explicit coercivity constant after the nonnegative perturbation.

findings.evidence: `UniformEllipticEnergy.lean:165`, `:177`, `:194`, and `:207` provide the existing explicit point/domain wrappers; `rg -n "UniformlyEllipticOn.*add.*energyIntegrand|isCoercive_energyIntegrand.*add" code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE` has no perturbed wrapper hits beyond `CoercivePerturbation.lean:66`.
findings.fix: Add `UniformlyEllipticOn` point and coefficient-field `_on` wrappers for the perturbed coercivity/lower-bound statements, mirroring `UniformEllipticEnergy.lean`.
findings.issue: The new perturbation API has no `UniformlyEllipticOn` point or `_on` wrappers, forcing roadmap callers with the named ellipticity hypothesis to manually unpack `h.lower_bound` and rebuild the raw theorem.

summary: The perturbation surface is too thin for the stated PDE consumers: it only transfers existential coercivity and leaves callers without the explicit estimate and bundled ellipticity wrappers used by the surrounding API.
